calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-06-2013 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-05-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-05-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Martin Vegas And Dpr Construction Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KEN LAU, ESQ., 415.433.1700, FOR PLAINTIFF DOUGLAS RUSSELL.
MARGARET MURRAY, ESQ., 415.585.5400, FOR DEFENDANTS MARTIN VEGAS AND DPR CONSTRUCTION.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendants Martin Vegas And Dpr Construction Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The Court denies the motion for summary adjudication as to the FEHA causes of action (causes of action 1-7 and 11-13) because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether a special employment relationship existed between plaintiff and defendant DPR. The evidence shows that: (1) Mr. Smith was plaintiff's supervisor on the site (Plaintiff's depo. 22, 38; Vegas depo. 27-28); (2) DPR needed to sign plaintiff's time cards for him to get paid (Plaintiff's depo. 96); (3) Mr. Vegas instructed Plaintiff on how to operate the elevator (Plaintiff's depo. 89-90; 153); (4) DPR provided locks for the elevator (Plaintiff's depo. 153); and (5) DPR set the work hours (Vegas depo. 136). "The common and prevailing principle espoused in all of the tests directs us to consider the 'totality of circumstances' that reflect upon the nature of the work relationship of the parties, with emphasis upon the extent to which the defendant controls the plaintiff's performance of employment duties." Vernon v. State of CA (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 114, 124. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the claim for negligent retention as to defendant DPR. Plaintiff's deposition testimony at 93-96 and par. 4 of the Chisum declaration show that the abuse was pervasive and there is a triable issue of fact regarding whether defendant ought to have been aware of it. In addition, that evidence shows that plaintiff was instructed not to report incidents to his supervisor Mr. Smith. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to defendant Vegas because he was not the employer and cannot retain himself. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the battery cause of action because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether ripping a back brace off of a disabled individual constitutes an offensive touching. (Plaintiff's depo. 92). Defendant fails to shift the burden on the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Plaintiff testified regarding severe emotional distress. (Plaintiff's depo. 165). The Court denies summary adjudication on the Unruh Act claim. If there is no employer-employee relationship between plaintiff and DPR, then the Unruh Act claim is an alternative remedy. See Payne v. Anaheim Memorial Medical Center 130 Cal.App.4th 729. Order signed in open court. Judge: Paul J. Alvarado, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MARTIN VEGAS & DPR CONSTRUCTION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to August 9, 2013, on the Court's motion. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.
